reasoning: Based on Federal Reserve Economic Data (DTB6 series), the 6-month Treasury bill rate declined from 4.33% (Oct 2024) to 3.65% (Oct 24, 2025), with a clear downward acceleration in Q3-Q4 2025. Given this strong downward momentum and monetary policy inertia, the probability of an increase from the 2025-10-26 baseline value is low for the 7-day horizon. However, uncertainty increases with forecast horizon as policy reversals become more likely over longer periods. For 2026-01-24 (~90 days), some chance of Federal Reserve policy shift exists.
